title: Reconstrainable stent delivery system with an annular lock and method
abstract: Devices, systems and methods are described and involve deploying and reconstraining a stent. An intravascular device with first and second elongated tubular members capable of axial motion relative to a housing having an annular lock configured to secure an expandable intraluminal device in first state and release the intraluminal device in second state, the intraluminal device being coaxialiy disposed over the second elongated tubular member proximal of a distal stop. Actuators on the housing may withdraw or advance the first and second tubular members. The first tubular member is withdrawn to deploy the stent and is advanced to reconstrain the stent. The second tubular member is withdrawn to engage the distal stop with the annular lock prior to reconstraining. The device may have a directional control that is engaged by an actuator to al low only proximal motion of the first elongated tubular member relative to the housing and allows only distal motion of the first elongated tubular member when disengaged.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An intravascular device comprising: a housing; a first elongated tubular member extending from the housing and capable of axial motion relative to the housing; a second elongated tubular member extending from the housing and capable of axial motion relative to the housing having a distal end with a radially projecting distal stop, wherein the second elongated tubular member is coaxially disposed within the first elongated tubular member; and an annular lock configured to secure an expandable intraluminal device in first state and release the intraluminal device in second state, wherein the annular lock is coaxially disposed over the second elongated tubular member and is coaxially disposed within the first tubular member and wherein the annular lock has an inner diameter that is less than an effective outer diameter of the distal stop. 2. The intravascular device of claim 1, further comprising a first actuator on the housing coupled to the first elongated tubular member that is configured to axiaily move the first elongated tubular member relative to the housing when manipulated, 3. The intravascular device of claim 2, wherein the first actuator is configured to axiaily move the first elongated tubular member until a distal end of the first elongated tubular member is positioned proximally relative to the annular lock. 4. The intravascular device of claim 2, wherein the first actuator comprises a rotary knob and wherein rotation of the knob axiaily moves the first elongated tubular member relative to the housing. 5. The intravascular device of claim 4, wherein the rotary knob and the first elongated tubular member are coupled to a flexible loop member. 6. The intravascular device of claim 5, further comprising a third actuator on the housing coupled to the flexible loop member. 7. The intravascular device of claim 2, further comprising a second actuator on the housing coupled to the second elongated tubular member that is configured to axiaily move the second elongated tubular member relati ve to the housing when manipulated, 8. The intravascular device of claim 7, wherein the second actuator has a first position relative to the housing that corresponds to a predetermined distal position of the distal stop relative to the housing and range of travel from the first position that axiaily moves the distal stop proximally relative to the predetermined distal position. 9. The intravascular device of claim 8, wherein the second actuator comprises a slider. 10. The intravascular device of claim 9, wherein the slider is disposed rearward of the first actuator closer to a user of the device. 11. The intrav ascular device of claim 8, further comprising a directional control associated with the first actuator, wherein the second actuator engages the directional control when in the first position such that the directional control allows only proximal motion of first elongated tubular member relative to the housing. 12. The intravascular device of claim 11, wherein the second actuator disengages the directional control when moved through the range of travel such that the directional control allows only distal motion of the first elongated tubular member relative to the housing. 13. The intravascular device of claim 12, wherein the directional control comprises a ratchet and pawl mechanism. 14. The intravascular device of claim of claim 7, wherein a cover is disposed over the second actuator such that access to the second actuator is gained by removal of the cover, 15. The intravascular device of claim 1, further comprising a proximal stop disposed within the first elongated tubular member at a fixed distal position relative to the housing, wherein the annular lock is distal to the proximal stop. 16. The intravascular device of claim 2, wherein the first actuator provides a mechanical advantage such that the corresponding axial movement of the first elongated tubular member relative to the housing is less than the input movement. 17. The intrav ascular device of claim 1 , wherein the annular lock comprises a plurality of recesses configured to capture elements of the expandable intraluminal device. 18. The intravascular device of claim 1, further comprising a frictional actuator coaxially disposed over the first elongated tubular member distal of the housing. 19. The intravascular device of claim 1 , further comprising a third elongated t bular member coaxially disposed within the second elongated tubular member and extending from the housing past the distal stop, wherein the third elongated tubular member comprises a lumen, a proximal port in fluid communication with the lumen and a distal portion having a plurality of perforations in fluid communication with the lumen. 20. The intravascular device of claim 19, wherein the intravascular device comprises a tapered distal tip having a plurality of flutes configured to allow egress of fluid supplied from the perforations of the third elongated tubular member when the distal tip abuts a distal end of the first elongated tubular member. 21. A reconstrainable stent deliver} 7 system comprising: 

 a housing; a first elongated tubular member extending from the housing and capable of axial motion relative to the housing; a second elongated tubular member extending from the housing and capable of axial motion relative to the housing having a distal end with a radially projecting distal stop, wherein the second elongated tubular member is coaxially disposed within the first elongated tubular member; an annular lock coaxially disposed over the second elongated tubular member and coaxially disposed within the first tubular member, wherein the annular lock has an inner diameter that is less than an effective outer diameter of the distal stop; and a self-expanding stent secured to the annular lock in first state and constrained in a reduced diameter within a distal portion of the first elongated tubular member. 22, A method for deploying and reconstraining an expandable intraluminal device comprising: providing an intravascular device having a housing, a first elongated tubular member extending from the housing, a second elongated tubular member coaxially disposed within the first elongated tubular member and extending from the housing with a radially projecting distal stop at a distal end of the second elongated tubular member, and an annular lock releasably securing an expandable intraluminal device, wherein the annular lock is coaxially disposed over the second elongated tubular member and is coaxially disposed within the first tubular member and wherein the annular lock has an inner diameter that is less than an effective outer diameter of the distal stop; positioning a distal end of the intravascular device at a desired location within a patient's vasculature; and axially moving the first elongated tubular member proximally relative to the housing so that a portion of the expandable intraluminal de vice is exposed. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein axially moving the fi rst elongated tubular member proximally relative to the housing comprises manipulating a first actuator on the housing that is coupled to the first elongated tubular member. 24. The method of claim 22, wherein axiaily moving the first elongated tubular member proximally relative to the housing comprises manipulating a slider on the housing coupled to the flexible loop member. 25. The method of claim 22, further comprising manipulating a second actuator on the housing that is coupled to the second elongated tubular member to axiaily move the second elongated tubular member proximally relative to the housing until the distal stop engages the annular lock. 26. The method of claim 25, further comprising axiaily moving the first elongated tubular member distallv relative to the housing to reconstrain the expandable intraluminal device. 27. The method of claim 26, further comprising applying force to the second actuator while axiaily moving the first elongated tubular member distallv to keep the distal stop engaged with the annular lock. 28. The method of claim 25, wherein manipulating the second actuator moves the second actuator from a first position relative to the housing that corresponds to a predetermined distal position of the distal stop relati v e to the housing through a range of travel from the first position and axiaily moves the distal stop proximally relative to the predetermined distal position to engage the annular lock. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the intravascular device comprises a directional control associated with the first actuator and wherein the second actuator engages the directional control when in the first position such that the directional control allows the first actuator to axiaily move the first elongated tubular member only proximally relative to the housing, further comprising disengaging the second actuator from the directional control such that the directional control allows only distal motion of the first elongated tubular member relative to the housing. 30. The method of claim 29, further comprising manipulating the second actuator to return to the first position and engage the directional control to allow only proximal motion of the first elongated tubular member relative to the housing. 31. The method of claim 25, further comprising accessing the second actuator by removing a cover disposed over the second actuator so that the second actuator is visible when the cover is removed. 32. The method of claim 26, further comprising manipulating a frictional actuator coaxialh/ disposed over the first elongated tubular member distal of the housing when axially moving the first elongated tubular member distal ly relative to the housing. 33. The method of claim 22, wherein the intravascular device further comprises an inner elongated tubular member coaxialh/ disposed within the second elongated tubular member and extending from the housing past the distal stop, wherein the inner elongated tubular member comprises a lumen, a proximal port in fluid communication with the lumen and a distal portion having a plurality of perforations in fluid communication with the lumen, further comprising supplying irrigation fluid to the proximal port prior to positioning the distal end of the intravascular device at the desired location to cause the irrigation fluid to flo from the plurality of perforations. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the intravascular device comprises a tapered distal tip having a plurality of flutes and wherein supplying irrigation fluid to the proximal port causes the irrigation fluid to be communicated through the perforations of the third elongated tubular member and flow out the flutes when the distal tip abuts a distal end of the first elongated tubular member.
